record.field: Infisical is an open-source platform to centrally manage application secrets, certificates, and privileged access across teams and infrastructure.

Infisical is an open-source platform to centrally manage application secrets, certificates, and privileged access across teams and infrastructure.

It replaces scattered .env files and ad-hoc credential sharing with a single source of truth for secrets, organized by project and environment. Built for developers and platform teams, it adds versioning, point-in-time recovery, role-based access control, and audit logging on top of a stack that is simple to self-host on PostgreSQL and Redis. It stands out for combining secrets management with secret scanning, dynamic short-lived credentials, and a private PKI in one platform.

Key features
  • Centralized dashboard for secrets across projects and environments, with versioning and point-in-time recovery
  • Secret syncs and native CI/CD integrations (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins, CircleCI), plus Terraform and Ansible
  • Kubernetes operator and agent to inject secrets into workloads without changing application code
  • Dynamic, short-lived credentials for databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Oracle and more)
  • Secret scanning and leak prevention covering 140+ secret types in files, directories and git repositories
  • Role-based access control, audit logging, and a built-in private PKI for issuing and managing X.509 certificates
Best for

Development and platform/DevSecOps teams that need a self-hosted, auditable source of truth for application secrets, database credentials, and internal certificates, and want an open-source alternative to HashiCorp Vault or Doppler.

field.name: tag_ids
field.eval: [Command.set([ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_self_hosted'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_security'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_privacy'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_identity'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_dev_tools'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_ci_cd'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_automation'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_secrets')])]

record.field: services: db-migration: depends_on: db: condition: service_healthy image: infisical/infisical:v0.135.0-postgres environment: - NODE_ENV=production - ENCRYPTION_KEY - AUTH_SECRET - SITE_URL - DB_CONNECTION_URI=postgres://{{ POSTGRES_USER }}:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}@db:5432/{{ POSTGRES_DB }} - REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379 - SMTP_HOST - SMTP_PORT - SMTP_FROM_NAME - SMTP_USERNAME - SMTP_PASSWORD - SMTP_SECURE=true command: npm run migration:latest pull_policy: always container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_db_migration' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal backend: restart: unless-stopped depends_on: db: condition: service_healthy redis: condition: service_started db-migration: condition: service_completed_successfully image: infisical/infisical:v0.135.0-postgres pull_policy: always environment: - NODE_ENV=production - ENCRYPTION_KEY - AUTH_SECRET - SITE_URL - DB_CONNECTION_URI=postgres://{{ POSTGRES_USER }}:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}@db:5432/{{ POSTGRES_DB }} - REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379 - SMTP_HOST - SMTP_PORT - SMTP_FROM_NAME - SMTP_USERNAME - SMTP_PASSWORD - SMTP_SECURE=true container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8080' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network redis: image: redis:7.4.1 env_file: .env restart: always environment: - ALLOW_EMPTY_PASSWORD=yes volumes: - ./redis_infisical_data:/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_redis' networks: - internal db: image: postgres:14-alpine restart: always environment: - POSTGRES_PASSWORD - POSTGRES_USER - POSTGRES_DB volumes: - ./pg_infisical_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data healthcheck: test: pg_isready --username={{ POSTGRES_USER }} && psql --username={{ POSTGRES_USER }} --list interval: 5s timeout: 10s retries: 10 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_db' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T db-migration sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d db-migration for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T db-migration sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T db-migration sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T db-migration sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
